commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces Codex cost-cache metadata/root checks with a stat-based metadata pass, skips duplicate known-existing path checks, preserves the v0.33 producer key as compatible, updates the generated parser hash, and adds focused regressions.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence local reproduction from this review: the linked issue contains concrete current-main 365-day archive timing, but I did not run that dataset or the PR branch. Source inspection does identify the repeated metadata/root-check path the PR targets.

target.title: perf: reduce Codex cost refresh metadata work
target.body:
## Summary

- replace separate Foundation metadata and resource-identifier reads with one portable `stat` call
- avoid repeated existence/root checks for Codex session paths already found during enumeration
- preserve the previous 0.33 Codex cache producer key because parsing semantics did not change
- add metadata lifecycle and cache compatibility regressions

Closes #1392.

## Proof

- `swift test --filter 'CostUsageCacheTests|CostUsageScannerTests'` — 21 tests pass
- `make check` — SwiftFormat and SwiftLint clean across 1,049 files
- `Scripts/regenerate-codex-parser-hash.sh check` — generated hash current
- `git diff --check` — clean
- Codex autoreview (`gpt-5.5`) — clean, no accepted/actionable findings; correctness confidence 0.82

The issue already contains real 365-day archive timing and sampling proof. This patch targets the repeated metadata/root-inspection work on that measured warm-refresh path without changing refresh policy or parsing behavior.
target.author: steipete
